kept.text:
Interesting…

According to GFHK’s analysis, low TSMC SoIC yields have led the industry to lower its near-term — i.e., 2026 — CPO shipment forecasts.

For what it’s worth, I was a bit disappointed that Teradyne seemed to acknowledge Advantest’s capabilities during this earnings call.

I had thought Teradyne would be overwhelmingly dominant in CPO testing. Now I’m debating whether I should add more Advantest.

quoted_tweet.text:
[GF Overseas Electronics & Communications] 
Teradyne 1Q26 Review: Entering a Transition Period in 2H26

☄️ Teradyne 1Q26 Review: Entering a Transition Period in 2H26

☀️ Strong 1Q results, but full-year 2026 guidance fell short of expectations:
Teradyne reported 1Q26 revenue of US$1.28 billion and gross margin of 60.9%, beating market expectations. Despite the strong first-quarter performance, the company guided 2Q26 revenue of US$1.15–1.25 billion, with the midpoint implying a 6% QoQ decline.
Management noted that 55–60% of 2026 revenue will still be concentrated in 1H26, which is below the market’s optimistic expectations for CPO, memory, and AI businesses. Meanwhile, in the CPO testing segment, management expects the medium-term TAM to reach US$300–700 million.

After the earnings release, the stock fell 20%. Although we remain constructive over the long term on TER’s AI-related share gains and CPO growth potential, near-term catalysts are limited. The company currently trades at 47x FY2026 P/E, representing a valuation premium versus SPE peers. Given uncertainty around 2H26 revenue, we lower our FY27 earnings multiple to 30x P/E and set a new target price of US$381.

☀️ 2H26 enters a transition period:
We believe the high first-half revenue weighting is partly due to the Mellanox business, where all 160–170 equipment shipments are concentrated in 1H26. Looking ahead, 2H26 will be a transition period for Teradyne’s business, with AI-driven growth expected to materialize more meaningfully in 2027.

Although Teradyne completed its first GPU tester shipments in 1Q26, these were mainly for Nvidia gaming GPUs. Testers for Nvidia’s Rubin platform are expected to begin shipping in 4Q26, with 2027 shipments ramping to 100–150 units. On the MediaTek side, a small number of tools will ship in 2026, while a more significant revenue contribution is expected in 2H27 as MediaTek’s Humufish capacity scales up.

☀️ CPO revenue has upside potential:
Management stated that the medium-term TAM for CPO test equipment is US$300–700 million, and it is also positive on providing Insertion 2 testing solutions through its partnership with Ficontec.

We are more optimistic on the outlook for the CPO business. According to supply-chain checks, Teradyne’s 2027 CPO revenue could reach US$800 million. The company has an absolute competitive advantage in full-flow CPO testing, covering Insertion 1/2/4 stages.

The current bottleneck in the CPO industry is concentrated in the OE stage, where SoIC process yields remain low, leading the industry to revise down near-term CPO shipment expectations. We recommend closely tracking CPO industry progress, as this business represents Teradyne’s biggest potential upside driver in 2026.

$TER

kept.text:
$TER

Great reaction to the earnings bomb yesterday. See if it can build out above 10WMA now. 

Sometimes when I have a lot of cushion I like to give them chance to reclaim key levels. Within reason of course, if it was down 40% yesterday then that's different. 
And within the context of a strong stock in a strong market. 

Sometimes it doesn't work and you'll sell a little lower, but other times they'll shake out those key levels and reclaim.

This is also a great example of what @801010athlete  talks about a lot; observing how individual stocks behave. 
You can see with the arrows that this has a habit of a big red day closing near lows and then bouncing straight back the next day. Yesterday was exaggerated with earnings but same principle as recent examples.

kept.text:
【GF 海外電子通訊】
Teradyne 1Q26 評論：2026 年下半年進入轉型期

- 1Q 業績強勁，但 2026 全年指引低於預期
Teradyne 公布 1Q26 營收達 12.8 億美元，毛利率 60.9%，優於市場預期。儘管第一季表現強勁，公司指引 2Q26 營收為 11.5–12.5 億美元，中值季減約 6%。 管理層指出，2026 年全年營收仍有 55–60% 集中在上半年，低於市場對 CPO、記憶體及 AI 業務的樂觀預期。在 CPO 測試領域，管理層預估中期 TAM 約 3–7 億美元。 財報發布後股價下跌 20%。雖然我們長期仍看好 TER 在 AI 相關市占提升及 CPO 成長潛力，但短期催化劑有限。目前本益比為 FY2026 47 倍，相較同業呈現估值溢價。考量 2H26 營收的不確定性，我們將 FY27 獲利倍數下調至 30 倍，目標價調整至 381 美元。

- 2026 年下半年進入轉型期
我們認為上半年高營收比重，部分來自 Mellanox 業務（160–170 台設備全數集中在上半年出貨）。展望下半年，Teradyne 將進入業務轉型期，AI 帶動的成長預計要到 2027 年才會更明顯實現。 雖然 Teradyne 已於 1Q26 完成首批 GPU 測試機出貨，但主要為 Nvidia 遊戲 GPU。Nvidia Rubin 平台的測試機預計 4Q26 開始出貨，2027 年出貨量將擴增至 100–150 台。在 MediaTek 部分，2026 年僅有少量設備出貨，較顯著的營收貢獻預計要到 2027 下半年，隨著 MediaTek Humufish 產能擴張而來。

- CPO 營收具上檔空間
管理層表示，CPO 測試設備中期 TAM 為 3–7 億美元，並對與 Ficontec 合作提供的 Insertion 2 測試解決方案持正面態度。 我們對 CPO 業務前景更為樂觀。根據供應鏈查核，Teradyne 2027 年 CPO 營收有機會達到 8 億美元。公司在全流程 CPO 測試具絕對競爭優勢，涵蓋 Insertion 1/2/4 階段。 目前 CPO 產業瓶頸集中在 OE 階段，SoIC 製程良率仍低，導致產業下修近期 CPO 出貨預期。我們建議密切追蹤 CPO 產業進展，此業務將是 Teradyne 2026 年最大的潛在上檔動力。

kept.text:
I always thought the acquisition of ABB Robotics (a leading high-precision robotics co) for ~$5.4B by SoftBank was quite interesting, and maybe the right approach to doing large-scale robotics with newer AI models. 

ABB Robotics makes $2.3B/year, so a ~2.3x multiple seemed pretty good, and very strategic. 

Another interesting company in the space would be Universal Robots, but they got acquired by Teradyne (which makes testing equipment for semiconductors) for $ 285M in 2015, which has worked out pretty well for them:

profile_bio.description: Researching LLMs | Roboticist | Prev: Partner @a16z (inv: @cursor_ai, @thinkymachines, @bfl_ml, @WaveFormsAI, @deeptuneai, etc), cofounder @Fellow_AI

quoted_tweet.text:
JUST IN: SoftBank is creating a brand new company — Roze AI, that uses autonomous robots to BUILD data centers.

And Masayoshi Son is already eyeing a $100 BILLION IPO by the second half of 2026.

The logic is simple. The AI boom requires data centers. Data centers require construction. Construction is slow, expensive and labour-intensive.

So why not automate the thing that builds the thing that runs the AI?

This is infrastructure automation at a scale nobody has attempted before. Robots building the server farms that power artificial intelligence. The whole stack, automated from the ground up.

The timing is aggressive, even some inside SoftBank are sceptical about the $ 100bn valuation and the IPO timeline.

But Son has never been known for thinking small.

For context, Jeff Bezos is reportedly running a similar playbook with Project Prometheus, a startup buying up industrial companies and modernising them with AI.

The world's biggest investors are all arriving at the same conclusion: the next trillion-dollar opportunity is not in software. It's in automating the physical world.

~~ 

♻️ Join the weekly robotics newsletter, and never miss any news → https://t.co/GoA3ZuwoPB

$AEHR It's been interesting to watch the stock the past week, following the pullback which $POET kicked off in the silicon photonics space.

$TER reported on Tuesday. Great numbers, but the stock dropped by double digits yesterday; a drop that $AEHR didn't participate much in. Teradyne is up again today.

Is the stock ahead of itself? Yes, but it's tough to fade this rally. The acceleration is clear, and it sounds like there's a strong chance of additional orders landing in May per the CEO. How much of that is priced in? We'll find out next month.
